task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Sample Order Lines' dataset is loaded into Oracle Analytics Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Oracle Analytics Desktop is open, and 'sample_order_lines2023' or 'Sample Order Lines' is visible in the data panel on the left.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a custom calculation named 'Unit Price'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A data element named 'Unit Price' is visible in the data panel under 'My Calculations', or a calculation editor window is open showing the formula 'Sales / Quantity' (or similar using the correct columns).

task_completion.description: Verify that the data is filtered for the 'Technology' Product Category.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A filter pill is visible in the filter bar at the top of the canvas showing 'Product Category' and the value 'Technology'.

task_completion.description: Verify that a scatter plot is created with Unit Price on the X-Axis and Quantity on the Y-Axis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A scatter plot visualization is present on the canvas. The X-axis label indicates 'Unit Price' and the Y-axis label indicates 'Quantity' (or 'Quantity Ordered'). The grammar panel should reflect these assignments.

task_completion.description: Verify that the scatter plot points represent individual Product Names.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Product Name' column is visible in the grammar panel for the scatter plot (e.g., under 'Category', 'Detail', or 'Color'), and the chart displays multiple data points corresponding to different products.

task_completion.description: Verify that a linear trend line is added to the scatter plot.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A straight line (linear trend line) is visibly overlaid across the data points on the scatter plot.

task_completion.description: Verify that the workbook is saved as 'Price_Elasticity.dva' in the Documents folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The top title bar of the Oracle Analytics window displays 'Price_Elasticity', or a 'Save As' dialog is visible showing the filename 'Price_Elasticity' and the directory 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Oracle Analytics Desktop GUI to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Oracle Analytics Desktop interface (menus, drag-and-drop, grammar panel) rather than terminal commands or code editors.

integrity.description: Verify the chart is a genuine Oracle Analytics visualization.
integrity.visual_evidence: The scatter plot is embedded within the Oracle Analytics canvas with standard UI controls (like the grammar panel and properties pane) active, not an external image opened in a viewer.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use a script to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows step-by-step manual creation of the calculation, filter, and chart within the application UI, rather than just executing a script that outputs a .dva file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected filename is 'Price_Elasticity.dva' saved in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents'. The custom calculation must use 'Sales' and 'Quantity'. The filter must be set to 'Technology'. The visualization must be a scatter plot with a trend line.
